patch.value: Lifecycle subcommand flags duplicated on the parent extension/package command are silently dropped (commander hoists them)

executeExtensionCommand reads command.opts() on the leaf lifecycle subcommand, but commander hoists options that are also defined on the parent command (e.g. --global/--project/--strict-exit/--fail-on-warn/--detail/--gh/--ref) onto the parent during parse. Result: 'pm extension doctor --strict-exit' or 'pm package explore --global' silently ignore the flag; only the top-level flag form ('pm extension --doctor --strict-exit') honors them. Repro: minimal commander program with duplicate parent/child options shows child opts() === {}. Found while writing tests/unit/commands/register-commands.spec.ts (see NOTE comment there). Fix options: read optsWithGlobals() in executeExtensionCommand, or stop duplicating the scope options on the parent.
